ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn does not have a specific concept for Hainanese Chicken Rice. Nearest parent is 'rice dish' (FOODON_03301712). A new concept proposal covering this Singapore-Chinese staple is warranted.
ontology.foodex2_description: Composite rice dishes — mixed rice dish with meat (closest parent)

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical plate of Hainanese Chicken Rice in Singapore weighs approximately 450g and consists of fragrant rice steamed in chicken broth with ginger and pandan leaf, topped with sliced poached chicken, served alongside a bowl of clear chicken soup, cucumber slices, and dipping sauces of chilli, ginger paste, and dark soy sauce.

nutrient_source.title: Hainanese Chicken Rice — HPB Singapore Foodo Database, Food Code EX000101
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Exact match found in HPB Foodo under food code EX000101 — Hainanese Chicken Rice, standard hawker preparation.

gi_source: University of Sydney GI Database — white jasmine rice (cooked, boiled); GI referenced for rice component; composite dish GI estimated based on rice proportion.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium approximately 1566mg per serving (450g) — about 68% of the 2300mg daily recommended limit, contributed primarily by soy-based dipping sauces, chicken broth, and seasoned rice.
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should request the dish without added dark soy sauce and limit the chilli sauce to reduce sodium intake to a safer level.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Jasmine rice base carries a medium-high glycaemic index (~58); per serving carbohydrate load is approximately 101g, which will produce a significant postprandial glucose rise.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Diabetic patients should halve the rice portion, increase the chicken and cucumber components, and prefer versions without the skin to reduce both carbohydrate and saturated fat load.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Poached chicken is a lean protein source with low inflammatory potential; ginger in the rice provides mild anti-inflammatory polyphenols.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Hainanese Chicken Rice is an acceptable meal choice for patients with rheumatoid arthritis; the lean poached chicken supports muscle maintenance and ginger may offer modest anti-inflammatory benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Hainanese Chicken Rice is Singapore's most iconic hawker dish, consisting of tender poached or steamed whole chicken served over fragrant rice cooked in seasoned chicken broth with ginger, garlic, pandan leaf, and sesame oil. The dish is accompanied by a clear chicken soup, sliced cucumber, and three dipping sauces — fresh chilli paste, grated ginger in chicken fat, and dark soy sauce. It traces its origins to Hainanese immigrants in Singapore and is considered a national dish, available at hawker centres, coffee shops, and dedicated chicken rice restaurants island-wide.

confidence_reason: Exact match found in HPB Singapore Foodo Database under food code EX000101 — Hainanese Chicken Rice, standard hawker centre preparation.
notes: Values represent the standard hawker centre preparation inclusive of rice, poached chicken, and accompaniments. Roasted chicken rice variants will have higher fat content. Sodium values are highly variable depending on quantity of dipping sauces used.
